title: senecio ferreyrae
intro: Senecio ferreyrae, also known as Andean wormwood, is a plant species native to the Andean region in South America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a bitter tonic and stimulate digestion. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever and soothe digestive issues. Senecio ferreyrae can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote digestive health and support the immune system.
